details.diagnosis: The task is to assess the likely AIE mechanism for the supplied molecule. From structure alone, the current top1 working hypothesis is ICT and the current top2 runner-up is TICT. A further still-credible portfolio member is neutral aromatic; ESIPT is presently weak because there is no clear proton-transfer motif, and unknown remains only a residual uncertainty label. Top1 is still uncertain because the present evidence is limited to structural priors: the molecule has a strongly substituted conjugated core suggestive of charge transfer, but it also contains several aryl rotors that could support TICT-like nonradiative channels, and its high aromaticity/compactness means a more neutral restriction-of-motion picture cannot yet be excluded. Shared prepared structure context is available and ready, including a selected conformer and geometry-derived proxies. Macro can realistically provide a deterministic registry-backed structural screening readout from the shared structure, but it cannot settle the global mechanism on its own. Microscopic in the first round can only do one low-cost baseline S0/S1 action on the shared structure, which can give indirect evidence about excited-state ordering and CT character but cannot yet test conformer sensitivity, torsional pathways, or full mechanism closure. Therefore the first round should gather both macro and microscopic evidence, and the case remains in portfolio screening rather than pairwise contraction because more than one non-top1 hypothesis is still credible and untested.
details.task_instruction: Run one deterministic macro screening pass on the shared prepared structure and one bounded baseline microscopic S0/S1 action on the same shared structure to collect indirect evidence that screens ICT against still-credible TICT and neutral aromatic alternatives.

agent_task_instructions.macro: Current reasoning phase: portfolio_screening. Agent framing mode: portfolio_neutral. The current_hypothesis 'ICT' is only a provisional top1 for bookkeeping in this stage. Do not anchor this macro task to it as if the mechanism were settled. Screening focus hypotheses: TICT, neutral aromatic. Screening focus summary: The case remains in portfolio screening because ICT is only the current structural favorite, while TICT and neutral aromatic both remain credible and have not yet been explicitly screened. Round-1 macro and bounded baseline microscopic work will provide indirect evidence on geometry preconditions and S0/S1 state character before any pairwise contraction. Task: Assess macro-level structural evidence relevant to the current working hypothesis 'ICT'. Reuse the shared prepared structure context when available, summarize low-cost structural indicators only, and stay within current macro capability. Hypothesis uncertainty to keep in mind: ICT leads only as a working hypothesis because structural push-pull features compete with substantial rotational freedom and a fairly rigid aromatic framework; direct excited-state evidence is still needed to screen TICT and neutral-aromatic alternatives.
agent_task_instructions.microscopic: Current reasoning phase: portfolio_screening. Agent framing mode: portfolio_neutral. The current_hypothesis 'ICT' is only a provisional top1 for bookkeeping in this stage. Do not anchor this microscopic task to it as if the mechanism were settled. Screening focus hypotheses: TICT, neutral aromatic. Screening focus summary: The case remains in portfolio screening because ICT is only the current structural favorite, while TICT and neutral aromatic both remain credible and have not yet been explicitly screened. Round-1 macro and bounded baseline microscopic work will provide indirect evidence on geometry preconditions and S0/S1 state character before any pairwise contraction. Task: Run the first-round low-cost S0/S1 microscopic baseline task for the current working hypothesis 'ICT'. Reuse the shared prepared structure context when available. Prioritize semi-empirical or otherwise low-cost bounded evidence collection, not heavy exhaustive geometry optimization. Report local microscopic results only, and do not attempt mechanism discrimination beyond current microscopic capability. Capability note: Current specialized-agent capability in round 1 is limited to indirect portfolio screening. Macro can supply deterministic shared-structure pattern analysis through one registry-backed capability, and Microscopic can supply exactly one low-cost baseline S0/S1 result on the prepared structure. Neither can by themselves fully resolve mechanism, perform multi-step discriminative workflows, or justify finalization in this initial round.

Macro can supply deterministic shared-structure pattern analysis through one registry-backed capability, and Microscopic can supply exactly one low-cost baseline S0/S1 result on the prepared structure. hypothesis_pool.name: ICT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Push-pull character is plausible from the zwitterionic quinonoid core and diarylamino/diaryliminium-like substitution, making charge-transfer-mediated AIE a leading working hypothesis.

hypothesis_pool.name: TICT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Multiple aryl rotors are present, so twisted charge-transfer deactivation remains credible, but the high planarity proxy and conjugated central core make it less clearly favored at the outset.

hypothesis_pool.name: neutral aromatic
hypothesis_pool.rationale: A rigid, highly aromatic, extensively substituted scaffold could still show AIE-like restriction of motion without dominant charge-transfer physics, so this remains a credible alternative.

hypothesis_pool.name: unknown
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Mechanistic assignment is still uncertain because only structure-level priors are available and no excited-state screening evidence has yet been collected.

hypothesis_pool.name: ESIPT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: The structure lacks an obvious classical intramolecular proton donor such as phenol or NH positioned for proton transfer, so ESIPT is presently weak.

hypothesis_screening_ledger.screening_note: Still credible because the molecule contains several aryl rotors; first-round macro and baseline S0/S1 can only indirectly constrain this.
Still credible because the scaffold is highly aromatic and fairly compact/planar, so non-CT restriction-of-motion behavior remains possible.
No obvious proton-transfer motif is visible from the prepared structure, so ESIPT is not a priority screening target in round 1.
Structured features do support named mechanisms better than a fully uncommitted label, but excited-state evidence is still pending.

planner_raw_response.portfolio_screening_summary: The case remains in portfolio screening because ICT is only the current structural favorite, while TICT and neutral aromatic both remain credible and have not yet been explicitly screened. Round-1 macro and bounded baseline microscopic work will provide indirect evidence on geometry preconditions and S0/S1 state character before any pairwise contraction.
planner_raw_response.screening_focus_summary: The next bounded actions should determine whether the shared prepared structure looks preorganized for charge transfer versus largely neutral aromatic emission, and whether the baseline S0/S1 result shows strong CT-like state signatures that indirectly weaken TICT or neutral-aromatic alternatives.

agent_task_instructions.Macro: Using exactly one registry-backed macro capability on the shared prepared structure, produce a deterministic structural screening report focused on geometry preconditions relevant to ICT, TICT, and neutral aromatic possibilities. Do not make a final global mechanism decision. Report only bounded observations such as apparent donor-acceptor partitioning, central-core versus rotor organization, overall planarity/compactness implications, and whether the visible motif strongly disfavors ESIPT.
agent_task_instructions.Microscopic: Run exactly one baseline-only S0/S1 action on the shared prepared structure under the standard low-cost route allowed in runtime context. Return a bounded report of the baseline S0 geometry/excitation findings relevant to state ordering, oscillator strength/brightness if available, and any coarse indication of charge-transfer versus more localized excitation character. Do not run conformer sensitivity, torsion sensitivity, follow-up optimizations, or multiple sequential Amesp actions. Do not make the global mechanism decision.

hypothesis_reweight_explanation.ICT: Rises because the conjugated zwitterionic/push-pull scaffold makes charge-transfer behavior structurally plausible.
hypothesis_reweight_explanation.TICT: Stays credible because multiple aryl rotors could enable twisted charge-transfer deactivation pathways.
hypothesis_reweight_explanation.ESIPT: Falls because no clear intramolecular proton donor-acceptor arrangement is evident.
hypothesis_reweight_explanation.neutral aromatic: Stays credible because the scaffold is highly aromatic and relatively compact, so non-CT restriction-of-motion AIE cannot yet be excluded.
hypothesis_reweight_explanation.unknown: Stays limited because named mechanisms already have plausible structural support, though evidence is not yet decisive.
